channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: FieldBuilder.ai files trademark for AI-powered insurance and financial services platform
item.description: The post FieldBuilder.ai files trademark for AI-powered insurance and financial services platform appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . FieldBuilder.ai has filed a trademark – signaling plans to offer a mix of insurance, financial advisory, and AI-driven software tools. The application, filed on April 15, 2026, outlines a broad platform spanning online financial planning, insurance brokerage advisory services, and coaching for financial professionals.

item.title: Moter Technologies files trademark for AI-driven motor insurance and risk platform
item.description: The post Moter Technologies files trademark for AI-driven motor insurance and risk platform appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . MOTER Technologies has filed a trademark application covering a new AI-powered platform focused on motor insurance, telematics, and claims analytics. Beyond software, the application references financial risk assessment services and tools aimed at fleet and autonomous vehicle insurance, pointing to a broader push into mobility risk management.

item.title: Rhino expands “Greenlight” push with deposit and rent-focused filings
item.description: Rhino filed a trademark for “GREENLIGHT DEPOSIT,” pointing to an expanded offering across security deposit alternatives, insurance, and property management tools. The filing comes alongside a separate “GREENLIGHT RENT” application, which expands the scope beyond deposits into rent payments, renters insurance, credit evaluation, and tenant screening services. On the technology side, the filing describes a suite of online tools for landlords and tenants to manage, finance, and pay security deposits, as well as access credit monitoring features.

item.title: India Approves $1.4 Billion Maritime Insurance Pool
item.description: India has approved a 129.8-billion-rupee ($1.4 billion) guarantee for a maritime insurance pool, a minister said on Saturday, as wars and sanctions prompt insurers to withdraw cover, threatening trade flows. The insurance pool will cover all maritime risks, including hull and machinery, cargo and war risk, the statement said. “There was a need for a domestic maritime risk covering pool to maintain sovereignty and continuity of trade in face of withdrawal of coverage due to sanctions or due to geopolitical tensions,” according to a statement issued by the government.

item.title: Markel launches FintechRisk+ in Australia
item.description: Markel has introduced its FintechRisk+ product in Australia, expanding its modular fintech insurance offering to a new market. FintechRisk+ builds on a product first launched in the London market in 2016 and later expanded across Europe, Canada, and Asia, where Markel reports significant growth in its fintech portfolio. Australia’s fintech sector, with more than 800 companies and strong projected growth, represents a key opportunity for specialty insurers looking to package liability and cyber risks into modular offerings.

item.title: Vertafore Advances ‘Distribution Velocity’ with AI Platform
item.description: Source: Vertafore.) At its Accelerate 2026 user conference in Las Vegas this week, Vertafore (Denver) has outlined a new AI-driven architecture and introduced a suite of workflow-focused agents aimed at accelerating what it describes as “distribution velocity” across the insurance value chain. The announcements, delivered during the conference’s general session by Amy Zupon, CEO, Vertafore, and James Thom, Chief Product Officer, center on the Velocity AI platform, an architectural layer designed to support the rapid development and deployment of AI capabilities across Vertafore’s portfolio. (Click to enlarge.) From Distribution Drag to Productivity Gains Vertafore’s response is what it calls “distribution velocity,” defined as the combination of speed and intentional outcomes.

item.title: WTW’s Trivedi Sees AI Driving Actuarial Productivity Gains
item.description: Life insurers are beginning to realize measurable productivity gains from artificial intelligence in actuarial and financial modeling, as the technology moves from experimental use cases into embedded workflow execution. Eddy Trivedi, Senior Director, WTW (London), describes a progression from early deployments delivering tangible efficiency improvements to a new generation of agentic systems capable of interacting directly with financial modeling platforms. “We believe AI is a tool, not a solution,” Trivedi comments.

item.title: Cyber threats zero in on Asia Pacific
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Transportation And Biotech Take The Lead
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . Transportation and biotech were leading themes among this week’s largest U.S.

item.title: Inszone acquires Insurance Resources Group in Kansas
item.description: Inszone Insurance has acquired Insurance Resources Group , a generalist agency based in Olathe, Kansas. Founded in 1996, Insurance Resources Group provides a broad range of insurance solutions to individuals and businesses, operating with a generalist approach rather than focusing on a single niche. The move expands Inszone’s presence in Kansas, continuing its strategy of growth through agency acquisitions.

item.title: Euclid Transactional announces 21 senior-level promotions
item.description: Euclid Transactional , a CRC Group MGA focused on transactional insurance underwriting and claims handling, announced 21 senior-level promotions across its North America, EMEA, and APAC operations as part of 47 title and role changes. Each of the 21 senior individuals promoted today is a leader in the transactional insurance industry, and we are proud of the service and knowledge they provide to our clients and carriers across the globe.” – Jay Rittberg, Managing Principal of Euclid Transactional. The senior-level changes span underwriting, claims, operations, and tax.

item.title: NFP acquires New Jersey-based Hamilton Group
item.description: NFP , an Aon company, has acquired The Hamilton Group , a multi-disciplinary broker based in Cedar Knolls, New Jersey. Hamilton Group serves small and mid-size businesses across New Jersey and the tri-state area and has operated for nearly 70 years. The agency has built longstanding carrier relationships across commercial and personal lines.

item.title: Innovación digital en la banca: líderes financieros se reúnen en Lima para una mirada regional
item.description: iupana anuncia la realización de iupanaDay 2026, un foro regional de banca digital e innovación financiera que reunirá el próximo 7 de mayo en Lima, Perú, a ejecutivos y líderes senior del sector financiero de América Latina para discutir, desde una perspectiva estratégica, cómo los datos y la inteligencia artificial están redefiniendo la eficiencia, la rentabilidad y los modelos operativos de la banca en la región. Concebido como un espacio privado de conversación entre pares, iupanaDay 2026 se posiciona como uno de los principales encuentros de innovación financiera en Latinoamérica, combinando análisis experto, networking curado y sesiones interactivas orientadas a la toma de decisiones ejecutivas. La agenda de esta edición sitúa a los datos y la inteligencia artificial en banca como eje central de la transformación.

item.title: Cork Cyber Launches Automated Mapping Capability
item.description: Cork Cyber (Boston) has introduced Auto Mapping, a capability within its Cork Vantage platform designed to automate the mapping of clients, devices and inboxes across integrations for managed service providers. Cork Cyber says the capability improves how assets are correlated across systems, supporting automation in areas such as risk analysis and remediation processes. “Automation only works if the underlying data model is solid,” says Dan Candee, CEO, Cork Cyber.

item.title: LexisNexis Risk Solutions Expands Agenium Integration
item.description: LexisNexis Risk Solutions (Atlanta) has expanded its integration with Agenium (Austin, Texas) to support life insurers’ access to medical data and underwriting workflows through digital platforms. Debra Gangelhoff, VP, General Manager, Life Insurance, LexisNexis Risk Solutions. The integration connects the LexisNexis Health Intelligence platform with Agenium’s no-code system, enabling insurers to request, manage and receive electronic health records and related data within existing workflows.

item.title: Guidewire HazardHub Finds Severe Storms Driving Losses
item.description: (Image credit: DerTobiSturmjagd/Pixabay.) Guidewire (San Mateo, Calif.) HazardHub has reported that severe convective storms are a primary driver of insured losses in the United States, with tornado, hail and flooding events contributing to increasing property damage and insurance claims. Guidewire HazardHub says these events frequently produce multiple hazards simultaneously, resulting in widespread property damage across large geographic areas. Guidewire HazardHub also reports a geographic shift in tornado activity toward the Southeast and Ohio Valley, increasing exposure in more densely populated regions.

item.title: Ford Recalling 1.4 Milion Pickup Trucks Over Gearshift Issue
item.description: Ford is recalling about 1.4 million F-150 pickup trucks in the U.S. Ford had earlier said that the issue may have been caused by electrical connections wearing down over time due to heat and vibration, leading to signal loss from the transmission range sensor. Related: Ford Recalling 420,000 Vehicles in US Over Windshield Wiper Failure The regulator said that incorrect signals likely lead to an unintended downshift.

item.title: Iran Says Hormuz Strait Now Completely Open for Commercial Ships
item.description: Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for commercial traffic, a major step toward ending a war with the U.S. “In line with the ceasefire in Lebanon, the passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire,” Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on X. Top photo: Cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz in February.

item.title: As Wisconsin Streets Flood, Midwest Braces for New Storms
item.description: Floodwaters from record rainfall in Wisconsin inundated streets on Wednesday, trapping drivers and forcing officials to close sections of a highway, as other Midwestern states worked to rebuild after storms. Meanwhile, communities in Michigan were recovering after powerful overnight storms damaged two ice arenas, flooded streets, and uprooted trees. The Milwaukee County Sheriff’s Office posted online to urge people not to drive in southeast Wisconsin.

item.title: More Than a Dozen U.S. States Consider Temporary Data Center Bans
item.description: The Maine bill, which still needs a final nod from Democratic Governor Janet Mills, would freeze approvals for data centers requiring more than 20 megawatts of power until October 2027, while a state-appointed council analyzes their impact on the local grid, electricity bills, air, and water. A dozen other states are considering a moratorium on data centers. Creates a statewide moratorium on data centers by prohibiting the operation of a data center unless the legislature first enacts rules, such as a statewide planning authority and explicit bans on shifting data center energy and water costs onto residential customers.

item.title: U.S. Weather Agency’s New Mobile Fleet to Provide Rapid Storm Insight
item.description: Three new mobile weather radars were unveiled by NOAA National Severe Storms Laboratory (NSSL) last month, allowing researchers to deploy research-grade technology to the front lines of tornadoes, wildfires, hail storms, flash flooding, and severe wind events, significantly expanding real-time insight into hazardous weather threats. These tools allow us to observe and document extreme hazards with a level of detail and confidence that was previously impossible.” The state-of-the-art mobile radars are designed to be rapidly deployed during dangerous weather, giving researchers detailed views of atmospheric processes that are difficult or impossible to capture with the agency’s fixed radar network alone. High-resolution radar data can reveal rapid changes in wind patterns and storm organization that influence storm strength and longevity, the agency stated.

item.title: Twelve Securis and Bank J. Safra Sarasin extend distribution partnership to Italy and Spain
item.description: Safra Sarasin to now include distribution across Italy and Spain. Safra Sarasin is broadening its alternative investment offering with the help of Twelve Securis, expanding its offering to clients in insurance-linked securities, while also expanding Twelve Securis’ distribution reach. Nils Ossenbrink, Managing Partner and Head of Distribution and Products at Twelve Securis, commented, “The collaboration between Bank J.

item.title: UK’s PRA to continue reforming ILS and cat bond frameworks to enhance competitiveness
item.description: The Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) of the Bank of England has confirmed that it will continue to work with the HM Treasury on further reforms to enhance insurance-linked securities (ILS) and catastrophe bond frameworks in the United Kingdom in order to amplify competitiveness within the market. In its announcement today, the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA), said: “During 2026/27, the PRA will continue to work with HMT on further reforms to ensure that the regulatory and legislative frameworks are aligned to support an internationally competitive sector while advancing the PRA’s objectives.” Meanwhile, in November 2025, the Prudential Regulation Authority also published a discussion paper to gather feedback on how the UK’s ISPV or ILS structures can play a role towards bringing in alternative capital to support life risks and reinsurance throughout the UK market. Today, the Prudential Regulation Authority published its business plan for 2026/27 and highlighted continued development of its insurance-linked securities (ILS) and alternative reinsurance capital frameworks.

item.title: James Sanzone appointed Head of Marine Cargo at Munich Re Specialty, NA
item.description: Global reinsurer Munich Re has announced the promotion of James Sanzone to Vice President, Head of Marine Cargo at Munich Re Specialty – North America (MRS-NA). In his new role, Sanzone will continue to lead the development of MRS-NA’s marine cargo offering and the expansion strategy of the team’s admitted capabilities for its Ocean Marine product line. He was later appointed Marine Cargo Underwriter, a role he held for two years before becoming Assistant Vice President, Ocean Marine, his most recent position.

item.title: AM Best revises Florida Farm Bureau Group’s outlook to positive
item.description: AM Best has revised the outlooks of Florida Farm Bureau Group’s members to positive from stable and affirmed the Financial Strength Rating of B++ (Good) and the Long-Term Issuer Credit Ratings of “bbb+” (Good). The revised ratings reflect Florida Farm Bureau Group’s balance sheet strength, which AM Best assesses as strong, alongside its marginal operating performance, limited business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management (ERM). AM Best noted, “In response to several years of volatile underwriting results, Florida Farm Bureau Group implemented significant rate increases, as well as deductible adjustments, non-renewals of certain classes of business, stricter underwriting guidelines and additional reinsurance coverage, all of which were aimed at improving profitability.

item.title: CEO John Doyle expects Marsh to be an ‘AI winner’
item.description: John Doyle, President and CEO of global insurance and reinsurance broking group Marsh, said he expects the firm to be an “AI winner,” as it leverages its scale and capacity to invest in artificial intelligence (AI) to drive even greater value, from its proprietary data assets to its role as a trusted advisor to clients. During the firm’s recent earnings call , Doyle outlined that Marsh’s AI strategy is focused on three core pillars: growth, productivity, and efficiency. AI-enabled savings will fuel additional growth investments, including in producer talent and new capabilities, building our confidence in continued margin improvement.” Doyle emphasised that AI will help Marsh serve clients with bespoke and complex needs even better, but it will not replace the trusted advice, expertise, and capabilities it delivers.

item.title: Marsh Aims to Be ‘AI Winner’ by Focusing on Gains in Growth, Productivity, Efficiency
item.description: Marsh aims to be an “AI winner” through growth, productivity and efficiency gains, according to John Doyle, president and CEO of the broker and risk adviser. He went on to detail Marsh’s strategy for its AI tech investments, which focuses on three main pillars: growth, productivity and efficiency. Following that measure of success, Marsh is focusing on scale in its AI investments and is adopting an enterprise-wide strategy.

item.title: Update: Shipping Firms Seek Clarifications Before Transiting Now-Opened Hormuz
item.description: Shipping companies have cautiously welcomed Iran’s announcement that the Strait of Hormuz is open but said they would require clarifications , including about the risk of mines, before vessels move through the entry point to the Gulf . “We are currently verifying the recent announcement related to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, in terms of its compliance with freedom of navigation for all merchant vessels and secure passage,” said Arsenio Dominguez, secretary-general of UN shipping agency the International Maritime Organization. Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said on Friday that the Strait of Hormuz was open to all commercial vessels during a 10-day Lebanon ceasefire accord, prompting a fall in oil and other commodity prices while stock markets rose.

item.title: State Farm Agrees to $15M Settlement for Underpaid Vehicle Claims in Arkansas
item.description: A federal judge last month approved a preliminary class settlement resolving claims that State Farm underpaid Arkansas policyholders for total loss vehicle claims by applying an erroneous valuation method. Under the settlement agreement, State Farm will pay $15.6 million to policyholders who filed a total loss claim between November 2016 and October 2021 and whose claim payment was based on an appraisal report from Audatex. Plaintiffs allege that State Farm used valuation reports prepared by Audatex that underrepresented the actual cash value of a loss vehicle by applying typical negotiation adjustments.

item.title: EIR Försäkring and Onuava launch fertility benefit in Germany
item.description: EIR Försäkring and Onuava have launched Fertility+, a corporate health insurance product for the German market focused on fertility and family building. The launch comes as fertility treatment remains only partially covered in Germany, leaving many couples to absorb significant out-of-pocket costs. EIR said the product reflects its strategy of building digital, needs-based insurance solutions, while Onuava is positioning the offering as a new type of employee benefit for employers looking to support workers earlier in the path to parenthood.

item.title: BerkleyNet expands workers’ comp coverage to Wisconsin
item.description: BerkleyNet has expanded its workers’ compensation offering to Wisconsin, adding the state as a primary coverage jurisdiction and enabling agents to quote and bind business directly through its platform. The move allows agents to support clients with multi-state operations across the Midwest under a single platform, while targeting industries such as manufacturing, construction, and logistics—key sectors in Wisconsin’s economy. The company can accommodate more than 400 class codes in the state and positions itself as an option for harder-to-place risks, including accounts transitioning from assigned-risk pools and new ventures without prior coverage.

item.title: We Kind of Wrote the Book on P&C Claims Correspondence
item.description: It is called The Secret History of Claims Correspondence , and we are publishing it free at voltaire.claims . Most of the correspondence rules in the book were drafted on an unstated assumption. Read it The Secret History of Claims Correspondence is free.

item.title: 100-Year Hurricane in NYC Could Cost Insurers More Than $100B: KCC
item.description: A 1-in-100-year hurricane event in New York City could cost insurers more than $100 billion, according to an analysis by Karen Clark & Co (KCC). And a 1-in-250-year hurricane could cost insurers twice as much, the catastrophe modeler says. The storm brought more than 15 feet of storm surge to Long Island and continued to track inland, causing damage well into Canada before transitioning to a post-tropical cyclone.

item.title: Chubb eyes Indian return as 100% FDI reshapes market access: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Agile promotes Mark Hunt to group CUO in underwriting leadership shake-up
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: XS Global, Sompo, Swiss Re, Marsh, Berkley Re: 14 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: HDH Investment Services Limited enters liquidation
item.description: On 16 April 2026, HDH Investment Services Limited (HDH), which advised on and arranged deals in investments, entered Creditors’ Voluntary Liquidation (CVL). If you do not hear from the joint liquidators or have more questions about your investment or pension, contact the Financial Services Compensation Scheme or the joint liquidators. Dina Devalia and Tom Parish of Quantuma Advisory Limited (Quantuma) have been appointed as joint liquidators.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Autonomous Vehicle Funding More Than Triples In 2026 To Hit Record Amount
item.description: Funding to autonomous vehicle startups has seen a massive resurgence in 2026, more than tripling so far this year compared to all of 2025 globally, Crunchbase data shows. So far in 2026, we’ve seen a shift to a small number of autonomous vehicle companies capturing a disproportionate share of global capital, with a handful of giants, including Waymo , Shield AI and Wayve , getting the lion’s share of funding. The numbers: Autonomous vehicle startups raised a record $21.4 billion across 34 deals through April 15, per Crunchbase data .

item.title: Investors show huge interest in data centre risk. Diversification story of ILS a consideration: SIFMA
item.description: Pawar stated, “It’s a complex subject matter, but I think, when we speak to our end-investor base, there’s a huge amount of interest in terms of this type of risk.” However, education is going to be critical as a new risk opportunity comes to the ILS market and Pawar highlighted that, “The kind of questions we’re being asked is, how are you looking at data centre risk, are you looking at cyber? During the SIFMA ILS conference in Miami yesterday a panel discussed the topic of data centre risk and some of the considerations that need to be kept in mind as the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market looks at these potentially meaningful opportunities. With the global data centre build-out continuing at pace and investments into their construction and financing growing, thoughts in the insurance and reinsurance market are on the enormous capacity opportunity to cover the significant risk limits being discussed, with ILS instruments seen likely to play a role, given the depths of capital market funds that could be deployed to support these facilities.

item.title: Turicum Re 2026-1 cat bond enables Zurich to re-establish its presence in growing ILS market: Mantero
item.description: Paolo Mantero, Head of Group Reinsurance for Zurich, commented: “Turicum Re enables Zurich to re-establish its presence and reputation in the growing and important ILS market. (Series 2026-1) issuance, which provides the firm with a multi-year and fully-collateralized source of US named storm and earthquake reinsurance protection, enables the company to re-establish its presence and reputation in the growing insurance-linked securities (ILS) market, Paolo Mantero, Head of Group Reinsurance has said. Initially, Zurich was looking to secure $125 million or more of multi-year and fully-collateralized US named storm and earthquake reinsurance protection from this Turicum Re 2026-1 cat bond deal.

item.title: Aon estimates Q1’26 global insured catastrophe losses of $20bn at least
item.description: Broking giant Aon has estimated that global insured catastrophe losses amounted to at least $20 billion in the first quarter of 2026, 6% above the 21st-century average, which came off the back of an economic toll of $37 billion, down sharply from $113 billion in the same period of 2025. Aon’s report also noted that natural catastrophes in the United States accounted for more than 75% of global insured losses in Q1 2026, reaching approximately $16 billion. According to the broker’s latest Global Catastrophe Recap, the largest contributors to the overall insurance toll were winter storms and severe convective storm (SCS) outbreaks across the United States.

item.title: Zurich closes $150m cat bond covering U.S. named storms and earthquakes
item.description: named storms and earthquakes, forming part of Zurich’s diversified natural catastrophe risk reinsurance strategy. Large insurer Zurich has returned to the catastrophe bond market, successfully closing a $150 million catastrophe bond covering U.S. named storms and earthquakes through the Turicum Re 2026-1 issuance .

item.title: Captive insurance identified as a financing mechanism supporting renewable energy growth: AXA XL
item.description: AXA XL, a global insurance and reinsurance company and part of the AXA Group, has published the report Future Energy Finance: How Captives are Powering Renewable Growth, which considers how the transition towards lower-carbon energy systems is increasing demand for adaptable risk financing approaches, with captive insurance arrangements highlighted as a key mechanism for supporting innovation and investment across the sector. The report also points to their role in enabling structured risk sharing across complex project arrangements, helping to align insurance and financial planning, as well as expanding available market capacity by supporting technologies as they progress from pilot schemes towards wider commercial deployment. Vicky Roberts-Mills, Global Head of Energy Transition at AXA XL, commented: “By 2030, global renewable capacity is expected to increase by 4,600 GW, with solar and wind technologies leading the charge, accounting for nearly 80% of new capacity.

item.title: Former Verisk CEO Stephenson joins ZestyAI’s Board of Directors
item.description: ZestyAI, a provider of AI-driven insurance risk analytics, has announced that Scott Stephenson, former Chairman, President, and CEO of Verisk Analytics, has joined its Board of Directors. Prior to Verisk, Stephenson was a Senior Partner at the Boston Consulting Group, where he advised Fortune 50 CEOs and founded the firm’s Southeastern U.S. Steph enson, who is considered one of the principal architects of the insurance industry’s data modernisation, is joining the company as the industry accelerates into the artificial intelligence era, ZestyAI highlights.

item.title: Europe Looks to Contain Trump’s Fury as It Hosts Hormuz Summit
item.description: The UK and France will host a summit to discuss setting up a naval force that would ensure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as Europe seeks to rebut US claims that it’s not doing enough to secure the waterway. Read More: US-Iran Deal Will Take Months, Gulf and Europe Officials Say The Paris meeting will be followed by a military planning summit in England next week and coupled with discussions with the insurance industry. The so-called Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative would be “strictly defensive in nature,” the UK said in a statement — underscoring how European leaders have no intention to help force a reopening of the Strait as President Donald Trump has demanded.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Will Take Months, Gulf and Europe Officials Say
item.description: Some Gulf Arab and European leaders believe that a US-Iran peace deal will take about six months to be agreed and that the warring sides should extend their ceasefire to cover that timeframe, according to officials from the regions familiar with the matter. Gulf states believe Iran is looking to build a nuclear weapon and that hasn’t changed in the wake of the US-Israeli bombardment of the country, said the officials. They thus think a peace deal should ban Iran from enriching uranium or having long-range ballistic missiles, the officials said.

item.title: Beazley Plans $1B Marine War Consortium for Transiting Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The consortium will be offered to vessels and their cargoes whilst transiting the Strait of Hormuz; coverage will be in line with Beazley’s risk appetite and in compliance with all global sanctions. Beazley, the specialty Lloyd’s insurer, has announced its intention to launch a marine war consortium, offering up to $1 billion of Lloyd’s capacity, which comprises $500 million for hull war and $500 million for cargo war. The consortium is designed to support the maritime sector with additional war insurance capacity as it deals with a complex and evolving situation in and around the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gallagher expands UK footprint with Bridge Insurance Brokers acquisition
item.description: has expanded its UK presence with the acquisition of Bridge Insurance Brokers Limited, reinforcing its position in key specialty sectors including real estate and construction. By integrating Bridge into its UK & Ireland Retail division, the broker is enhancing its ability to serve mid-market organisations with tailored risk and insurance solutions. Bridge brings more than five decades of experience and a strong regional footprint, along with established expertise in complex commercial lines.

item.title: Inszone strengthens specialty capabilities with Randall Realty & Insurance acquisition
item.description: The post Inszone strengthens specialty capabilities with Randall Realty & Insurance acquisition appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Inszone Insurance Services has continued its expansion strategy with the acquisition of Randall Realty & Insurance, a move that enhances its presence in the specialised manufactured home insurance market. As demand for tailored insurance solutions continues to rise, the acquisition reinforces Inszone’s commitment to expanding its specialty capabilities while delivering enhanced value and resources to clients.

item.title: VisGen files trademark tied to insurance and denial recovery services
item.description: Visgen LLC has filed a trademark for “VISGEN,” signaling plans to expand into insurance and healthcare-focused services. Founded in 2025, VisGen is building an AI-driven denial recovery platform targeting healthcare providers, particularly federally qualified health centers. The application, filed April 14, 2026, covers insurance claims auditing, data processing, and consulting, including eligibility verification and health insurance advisory—pointing to a move beyond pure AI consulting into operational insurance workflows.
